title: Website audit and enhancement
body: Fix build errors, enhance site navigation (header, footer, sidebar), and resolve broken links to improve site functionality and user experience. The project initially failed to build due to several TypeScript errors, including duplicate tags and prop type mismatches. These were resolved, and type safety was improved in service components. Additionally, the header, footer, and sidebar were significantly restructured to provide more comprehensive and organized navigation, addressing numerous broken links and adding missing routes for better site discoverability and user flow. ---

title: Expand services and deploy updates

The task explicitly requested a significant expansion of services with detailed advertising content, market prices, and contact information, which necessitated new data structures, dedicated pages, and navigation updates to properly present these new offerings. ---

title: Fix unexpected app opening error
body: Fix "Something went wrong" error by resolving Vite React plugin compatibility and invalid HTML. The error was caused by a `@vitejs/plugin-react` preamble detection issue, invalid JSX in `index.html`, and a missing `@babel/plugin-transform-react-jsx` dependency. This PR addresses these by removing the invalid JSX, installing the missing Babel plugin, and downgrading `@vitejs/plugin-react` to a compatible version (4.2.1) to ensure proper React Refresh functionality. ---

title: Check and fix github actions
body: Fixes and hardens GitHub Actions workflows by resolving YAML syntax errors, improving security, and standardizing configurations. This PR addresses various issues across GitHub Actions workflows, including: - **Security:** Switched `pull_request_target` to `pull_request` and added explicit `contents: read` permissions where appropriate. - **YAML Syntax:** Corrected numerous parsing errors such as stray characters, incorrect indentation, duplicate blocks, and improper quoting. - **Standardization:** Updated `actions/checkout` and `actions/setup-node` to `v4`, standardized Node.js versions, and improved script robustness (e.g., replacing heredocs). All workflows now pass YAML validation. ---

title: Check and fix github actions
body: Fix dependency issues and resolve merge conflicts to enable successful project builds and GitHub Actions. The project was failing to build due to a `tailwind-merge` version mismatch, an unnecessary `husky` prepare script, and numerous merge conflicts across several UI components and the `Services.tsx` page. These issues were preventing GitHub Actions from running correctly. This PR addresses these critical build blockers. ---

title: Build and fix errors
body: Resolve merge conflicts and fix build errors to restore a functional project state. The project was in a non-buildable state due to numerous merge conflicts across `package.json`, service worker files, and several core React components (`App.tsx`, `main.tsx`, `Home.tsx`, `button.tsx`, `card.tsx`). This PR addresses all identified conflicts and compilation errors, including a complete rewrite of `Home.tsx` to resolve its extensive conflicts, ensuring the project now builds successfully and the development server can start. ---

title: Build and fix errors
body: Configure ESLint for TypeScript/React project to enable successful linting and resolve parsing errors. The project's ESLint configuration was outdated, using the old format and lacking proper TypeScript and React support. This PR updates the configuration to the new flat config format, installs necessary plugins, adds TypeScript parsing, includes React globals, and sets up ignore patterns to focus linting on active source code, allowing the lint command to run without critical parsing errors. ---

title: Build and fix errors
body: This pull request contains changes generated by Cursor background composer.

title: Resolve typescript merge conflicts
body: Fix TypeScript compilation errors and data type mismatches to enable a successful project build. The project was failing to build due to a series of TypeScript issues, including incorrect JSX configuration, missing React type definitions, and type mismatches between the `MicroService` interface and the actual data in `src/data/comprehensiveServices.ts`. Additionally, Framer Motion `ease` properties were incorrectly typed, and several components were either missing or had incorrect import/export patterns. This PR addresses these issues by updating TypeScript configurations, refining data interfaces, correcting component imports, and adding placeholder components for previously missing ones, leading to a successful build. ---

title: Install project dependencies and husky
body: Update Netlify build command to use Yarn instead of npm to resolve dependency installation failures. The build was failing because `netlify.toml` specified `npm` commands, but Netlify was actually using `yarn install`. This mismatch caused the `husky install` script to fail with a "husky: not found" error during the Yarn installation process. This change aligns the build command with Yarn. ---

title: Build project and handle publish directory error
body: Update Netlify configuration to correctly build a Vite project by changing the publish directory and removing Next.js-specific plugins. The Netlify build was failing because the `netlify.toml` was configured for a Next.js project (using `@netlify/plugin-nextjs` and `publish = "out"`), while the actual project is built with Vite (which outputs to `dist`). This PR corrects the publish directory to `dist` and removes the Next.js plugin, an unused `netlify/functions` directory reference, and the `netlify-plugin-cloudinary` plugin to align the configuration with the Vite project structure. ---
